title: plan-loop tasks file (tasks.json)
description: The structured, executable output of the plan-loop: a first-principles decomposition plus PR-sized tasks ordered by dependency. tools/validate_plan.py enforces this contract and the dependency/coverage invariants on top of it.

objective.description: What the user wants and why — one crisp paragraph.

end_state.description: Definition of done: what observably exists/works when the plan is fully executed.

non_goals.description: Explicit scope boundaries — what this plan deliberately does not do.

test_command.description: How the project's tests are run, if known.

components.description: First-principles building blocks of the work; every task must serve at least one.

open_questions.description: Unknowns/decisions the human should resolve before or during execution.

tasks.description: PR-sized units of work (target ~100-400 LOC). Each is self-contained enough for a junior engineer or a smaller model to execute correctly.

description.description: What this task delivers and how it fits the end state.

subtasks.description: Atomic steps a junior can do without further decomposition, e.g. 'define function parse_config(path)'.

acceptance_criteria.description: Observable conditions that mean the task is done.

depends_on.description: task ids that must complete first.

estimated_loc.description: Rough lines-of-code estimate; PR-sized tasks target ~100-400.

suggested_skills.description: installed skills that could help execute this task.

order.description: A valid topological ordering of all task ids (each task after its dependencies).
